name: Pico Coriscáu from Puerto de San Glorio — Parque Nacional Picos de Europa
name_original: Pico Coriscáu desde el Puerto de San Glorio — Parque Nacional Picos de Europa
region_intro: Hiking around Vega De Liébana offers access to the dramatic mountainous terrain of Cantabria, primarily within the Picos de Europa National Park. The region features sharp peaks, deep ravines, and diverse vegetation blending Mediterranean and Atlantic species due to a unique microclimate. Hikers can explore hillsides with deciduous beech and Pyrenean oak, alongside evergreen holm oak, shaped by the valleys of the Deva, Bullón, and Quiviesa rivers. Best hiking trails around Vega De Liébana The most popular hiking route is Pico Coriscáu from Puerto de San Glorio — Parque Nacional Picos de Europa, a 7.8 miles (12.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 59 minutes to complete. This route offers incredible views of the Picos de Europa and other significant peaks. Another top favourite among local hikers is Hike from Collado de Llesba – Picos de Europa National Park, a difficult 5.3 miles (8.6 km) path. This trail provides expansive views within the Picos de Europa National Park, starting from a notable viewpoint. Local hikers also love the Las Praizas de Cucayo Loop, a 3.7 miles (5.9 km) trail leading through varied landscapes near the charming village of Cucayo, often completed in about 2 hours 1 minute. Hiking around Vega De Liébana is defined by dramatic mountainous terrain, deep river valleys, and extensive woodlands within the Picos de Europa National Park. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Vega De Liébana are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 280 reviews. More than 1,600 hikers have used komoot to explore Vega De Liébana's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Vega De Liébana?
A: Vega De Liébana offers a diverse range of hiking experiences with over 20 routes. These include 6 easy trails, 12 moderate routes, and 6 challenging paths, ensuring options for various skill levels within the dramatic landscapes of the Picos de Europa National Park.

Q: What are the general difficulty levels of hiking trails in Vega De Liébana?
A: The hiking trails in Vega De Liébana cater to a wide range of abilities. You'll find 6 easy routes, 12 moderate trails, and 6 difficult paths, allowing hikers to choose adventures that match their experience and fitness levels.

Q: When is the best time to go hiking in Vega De Liébana?
A: Vega De Liébana benefits from a unique microclimate, making it suitable for hiking across different seasons. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, while summer provides warmer conditions ideal for higher altitude trails. Winter hiking is possible, especially at lower elevations, but requires appropriate gear due to potential snow and ice in the Picos de Europa.

Q: Are there any circular walks in Vega De Liébana?
A: Yes, Vega De Liébana features several excellent circular walks. For a moderate option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001162887/las-praizas-de-cucayo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Praizas de Cucayo Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through varied landscapes near the charming village of Cucayo. Another moderate circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996738181/pico-jano-circular-from-dobarganes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Jano – circular from Dobarganes&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views. For a longer moderate loop, try the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001153864/pico-curiales-and-mediajo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Curiales and Mediajo Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Vega De Liébana?
A: While many trails in Vega De Liébana involve mountainous terrain, there are easier options suitable for families. The region offers 6 easy routes, often found at lower elevations or through more gentle woodlands, providing a good introduction to the area's natural beauty without overly strenuous climbs. Look for trails around picturesque villages like Cucayo for more accessible walks.

Q: What kind of natural features and landmarks can I see while hiking?
A: Vega De Liébana is rich in natural features and landmarks. You can explore dramatic mountain peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/176220" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jano Peak&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5923027" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Oviedo Summit&lt;/a&gt;, offering breathtaking vistas. The region is also known for its deep river valleys, extensive woodlands like the Castañar de Pendes, and fascinating rock formations. Don't miss viewpoints such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/280873" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Cable Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, accessible via the Fuente Dé Cable Car, for expansive panoramas of the Picos de Europa.

Q: Are there any trails that offer exceptional views of the Picos de Europa?
A: Absolutely. Many trails provide stunning views of the Picos de Europa. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1197740282/pico-coriscau-from-puerto-de-san-glorio-parque-nacional-picos-de-europa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Coriscáu from Puerto de San Glorio&lt;/a&gt; route offers incredible views of the Picos de Europa and other significant peaks like Peña Remoña and Peña Vieja. Another excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996915932/hike-from-collado-de-llesba-picos-de-europa-national-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hike from Collado de Llesba&lt;/a&gt;, which provides expansive views within the National Park itself.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Vega De Liébana?
A: The hiking trails in Vega De Liébana are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 280 reviews. Hikers often praise the dramatic mountainous terrain, the diverse vegetation, and the stunning views of the Picos de Europa National Park.

Q: Are there options for more advanced hikers seeking a challenge?
A: Yes, Vega De Liébana offers 6 difficult trails for experienced hikers. These routes typically involve significant elevation gains and rugged terrain, providing a true challenge within the Picos de Europa. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996915932/hike-from-collado-de-llesba-picos-de-europa-national-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hike from Collado de Llesba&lt;/a&gt;, known for its demanding ascent and rewarding views.

Q: Are there any specific points of interest or attractions near the hiking routes?
A: Beyond the trails, the region offers several points of interest. The Fuente Dé Cable Car provides quick access to high altitudes for further exploration. The Camino Lebaniego pilgrimage route also passes through the area, offering cultural insights. You can also visit charming villages like Cucayo and Potes, or explore impressive woodlands such as the Castañar de Pendes.

Q: Is there parking available at the trailheads?
A: Many popular trailheads in Vega De Liébana, especially those leading into the Picos de Europa National Park or starting from villages, typically offer designated parking areas. For example, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1197740282/pico-coriscau-from-puerto-de-san-glorio-parque-nacional-picos-de-europa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Coriscáu from Puerto de San Glorio&lt;/a&gt; often have parking facilities nearby. It's always advisable to check specific route details for parking information.

Q: Can I reach hiking trails using public transport?
A: While public transport options might be limited directly to all remote trailheads, the regional capital Potes serves as a hub with bus connections to some villages within Liébana. From these villages, you can often access various hiking routes. For more remote starting points, a car is generally recommended for flexibility.
tips: The monument to the bear is located in the Picos de Europa National Park and offers wonderful views in all directions. It is also very popular among cyclists since it is located meters from the Collado de Llesba port.

Also known as Coriscao, the top is at an altitude of 2,234 meters above sea level and the views are truly wonderful. With good visibility you will have incredible views of the Picos de Europa, the Fuente Dé cable car and the peaks of Peña Remoña, Peña Vieja and Torre del Llambrión.
